agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Session 198 SHIPPED US-317 passes:true (Sprint 30 / V0.27.4 P1 final story -- I-021 close, drive_summary writer decoupled from Ollama trigger + backfill script extension for NULL-drive_id legacy rows + missing-row INSERTs). PRE-FLIGHT confirmed only ONE call site couples writer to ping (enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync at analysis.py:1025). Fix is exactly the two-line shape per I-021 -- writer block (factory()/run_sync over _ensureDriveSummary) MOVED ABOVE the pingOllama gate; the create_task scheduling for-loop stays AFTER the gate so return-value semantic preserved. WARNING log message updated: 'Auto-analysis AI step skipped: Ollama unreachable at %s (device=%s, drives_written=%d). drive_summary rows still written.' Existing test_sync_auto_analysis.py 12 tests stay GREEN because (a) Ollama-down test only asserts analysis_history empty (not drive_summary), (b) WARNING-mentions-Ollama assertion still holds verbatim, (c) Failure-Isolation test mocks pingOllama=True so doesn't touch the changed gate. BACKFILL EXTENSION: 3 explicit paths -- Path A (existing post-US-200 UPDATE), Path B NEW (NULL-drive_id legacy rows scoped by device_id, uses row.start_time/end_time as legacy time-range filter, preserves natural-key boundaries, populates duration_seconds + row_count + is_real + data_source + profile_id only), Path C NEW (drive_ids in realtime_data without drive_summary row, uses _computeDriveAnalytics with sentinel datetime.min fallbacks because driveId-set short-circuits the time filter, INSERTs new row). BackfillStats gains `inserted: int = 0` field; main() log line includes inserted count. RED phase: 5 of 7 tests in test_drive_summary_decouple_from_ollama.py FAILED with the exact discriminator signatures designed: (1) drive_summary table empty when ollama-down (writer short-circuited), (2) backfill stats.populated=0 because pre-fix filter excluded NULL drive_id, (3) drive_summary table empty when missing-row case (no INSERT path existed), (4)+(5) AttributeError 'BackfillStats' has no attribute 'inserted' (structural failure -- field literally didn't exist). 2 of 7 PASSED pre-fix (Ollama-up happy path + no-boundaries early return -- regression-pins not discriminators; cleanly differentiates the new/regression test types). GREEN phase: 7/7 GREEN + adjacent cluster regression run (64 tests across test_sync_auto_analysis.py, test_drive_summary_12field_contract.py, test_drive_summary_reconciliation.py, test_drive_summary_sync.py, test_drive_summary_pi_shape_insert.py, test_drive_summary_sync_post_reconcile.py, test_drive_report_single_row.py) all GREEN + full fast suite GREEN (exit 0). Ruff clean across 3 modified files. sprint_lint 0/0/3. KEY DESIGN CHOICES: (1) writer-block-moved-above-gate cleaner than wrapping in conditional -- preserves the create_task return-value semantic (True iff at least one task scheduled). (2) BackfillStats `inserted: int = 0` default keeps back-compat for hypothetical callers but main() is only in-tree caller. (3) Path B preserves natural-key boundaries (start_time / end_time stay untouched) -- legacy rows are identified by (device_id, start_time) per _ensureDriveSummary's legacy lookup; touching them would break the natural-key contract. (4) Path C uses datetime.min sentinel for fallback args (sound under function contract: when driveId is set, fallbacks aren't read; type hint stays datetime not Optional). (5) Path B skips rows missing start_time/end_time and reports as 'skipped' so a human investigator can find them, rather than silently dropping. STOP CONDITIONS NOT TRIGGERED: writer/ping coupling localized to ONE call site (#1 not triggered); Path B + Path C use existing DriveSummary columns, no migration needed (#2 not triggered). NEW: tests/server/test_drive_summary_decouple_from_ollama.py (7 tests / 5 classes). FILES TOUCHED: 3 (1 src + 1 script + 1 test) -- exactly the sprint scope. Real-world validation gate per sprint validation block: post-V0.27.4 deploy + Drive 11+ produces server-side drive_summary row populated within 30s of sync round-trip REGARDLESS of Ollama reachability + backfill cleans drives 3-10 (CIO + Mike + Spool to confirm). EXIT <promise>COMPLETE</promise> -- Sprint 30 / V0.27.4 has 3/3 stories passes:true (US-315 sync UPDATE propagation + US-316 calibration.py PYTHONPATH bootstrap + US-317 drive_summary Ollama-decouple). Sprint done; ready for Mike's deploy ritual. Prior session (197): SHIPPED US-316 passes:true (Sprint 30 / V0.27.4 P1 -- I-020 close, calibration.py local-invocation PYTHONPATH bootstrap, third layer of I-018 not caught by V0.27.3 US-312). FIX: top-of-file sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent.parent)) block in src/server/analytics/calibration.py between `from __future__ import annotations` and the rest of the imports. Standard idiom for src/-layout repos. PRE-FLIGHT STOP-CONDITION #2 TRIGGERED + RESOLVED IN-SCOPE: rg calibration found 2 library importers (src/server/reports/drive_report.py:59 + scripts/report.py:74) contradicting story acceptance #1's `no other files import calibration.py as a module` claim. Spillover analysis: insertion is benign for library callers because they already have `src` resolvable as prerequisite for their own from-import line; sys.path.insert(0, repo_root) when repo_root already there creates duplicate that Python deduplicates at lookup. Per stopCondition's documented `accept the spillover` branch, accepted + documented in feedback.grounding rather than filing a blocker. RED phase: 2/2 tests in tests/server/test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py FAILED with the exact CIO 2026-05-10 stderr trace verbatim (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'` at line 58). Subprocess-based, scrubs PYTHONPATH from env, neutral cwd outside repo root, absolute script path with --calibrate --apply args. GREEN phase: 2/2 GREEN + US-312 existing test_calibration_cli_integration.py 4 tests GREEN + test_calibration.py 25 GREEN + test_migration_0008_baselines.py 25 GREEN (54 calibration-cluster tests, zero regressions). Smoke test (subprocess from TEMP cwd, no PYTHONPATH, absolute path): returncode=0 empty stderr. Ruff clean across both files. sprint_lint 0/0/3. KEY DESIGN CHOICES: (1) unconditional bootstrap (matches I-020 recommendation + standard src/-layout pattern) over `if __name__ == '__main__'` guard -- spillover empirically benign + guard would create script-vs-library sys.path asymmetry surface that's painful to debug. (2) underscore-prefixed `_bootstrapSys` / `_BootstrapPath` aliases inside the bootstrap block so future readers don't get confused that sys/Path are ambient names; the alias is the bootstrap-only marker. (3) two independent test assertions (returncode==0 AND marker-strings absent) -- a future bug could exit non-zero for a different reason; marker pin keeps I-020-specific signature visible. (4) test scrubs PYTHONPATH via env.pop('PYTHONPATH', None) instead of leaving any value -- mirrors CIO's actual local-Windows usage context exactly, the discriminator that US-312's PYTHONPATH=PROJECT_ROOT-set test missed. NEW: tests/server/test_calibration_cli_local_invocation.py (2 tests / 1 class). Files touched: 2 (1 src + 1 test). Next available: US-317 (M, P1, drive_summary writer Ollama-decouple, I-021). Unblocked, no inter-dependency. Single-agent mode (only Rex active); per single-agent decision tree, 1 unfinished story remains so emit NO tag and ralph.sh continues to next iteration. Prior session (196): SHIPPED US-315 passes:true (Sprint 30 / V0.27.4 P1 lead -- B-065 sync UPDATE propagation gap, the headline V0.27.4 bug). DESIGN: parallel modified_at cursor ADDITIVE alongside existing pk-monotone INSERT delta. Per-table opt-in via SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK = {battery_health_log, drive_summary, dtc_log}. Each opt-in table gets _sync_modified_at TEXT column + AFTER UPDATE trigger; sync_log gets last_synced_modified_at column. KILLER PRE-FLIGHT FINDING: server-side _upsertBatch (sync.py:386-409) ALREADY implements on_duplicate_key_update / on_conflict_do_update -- has supported UPDATE propagation since US-CMP-004; bug under B-065 was 100% Pi-side cursor mechanics. Server change collapsed to mod-history note. NEW: tests/pi/sync/test_update_propagation.py (8 tests / 3 classes). 6 files touched. Calibration_sessions has same UPDATE pattern (session.py:112) but out of sprint enumeration; one-line config addition post-V0.27.4. CONNECTION_LOG resolved as INSERT-only -- Spool's US-200 reference was stale. Full fast suite GREEN. Ruff + sprint_lint clean.

agents.name: Agent2
agents.note: Sprint 25 / Session 158 -- US-286 SHIPPED passes:true. tests/pi/integration/test_engine_drive_capture_e2e.py (1 class / 1 test / 7 phases): real ApplicationOrchestrator + DriveDetector + SummaryRecorder + ObdDataLogger; mock surface = connection.obd.query (BT edge) only; 0.16s call duration vs 30-sec budget. Mirrors US-260 cold-start pattern. Discriminator: would FAIL against May-4-5 production regression class (DriveDetector never operational) + against any silent realtime_data write regression. Two gotchas surfaced for future tests: (1) e2e fixtures must seed profiles('daily') because realtime_data has FK + bare orchestrator doesn't run ProfileManager; (2) production order query->logReading->_handleReading means first N ticks land with drive_id NULL -- query realtime_data without drive_id filter. Standing by post-US-286.
